# Eval Set Review

**STATUS: UNVERIFIED DRAFT.** These 40 cases were generated by reading the actual corpus documents, but have not been checked by a human. Please review each row: confirm the query is answerable from the linked doc(s) (or correctly labeled as tools/both/out_of_scope), the key facts are accurate, and the expected_route makes sense. Correct anything wrong directly in `eval/cases.json` (this file is just the human-readable view).

Total cases: 40 — 24 retrieval, 8 tools, 4 both, 4 out_of_scope.

| ✓ | ID | Query | Category | Expected Route | Relevant Doc(s) | Key Facts | Notes |
|---|----|-------|----------|-----------------|------------------|-----------|-------|
| ☐ | eval-001 | By how many degrees Fahrenheit did Webb observe the temperature of exoplanet HD 80606 b spike as it swept close to its star? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA's Webb Catches Exoplanet Getting Roasted](https://science.nasa.gov/missions/webb/nasas-webb-catches-exoplanet-getting-roasted/) | - Temperature increased by about 1,100 degrees Fahrenheit<br>- Observed with Webb's MIRI instrument<br>- Happened near periastron (closest approach to its star) | Easy, single-fact retrieval from a JWST exoplanet press release. |
| ☐ | eval-002 | Approximately how many individual stars did Webb's imaging survey resolve within the M82 Cigar Galaxy? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA’s Webb Pinpoints Millions of Stars Within Cigar Galaxy](https://science.nasa.gov/missions/webb/nasas-webb-pinpoints-millions-of-stars-within-cigar-galaxy/) | - About 16.5 million individual stars resolved<br>- Survey used 65 total hours of NIRCam observation time<br>- M82 is 12 million light-years away | Easy, single distinctive number. |
| ☐ | eval-003 | What is unusual about the atmosphere of exoplanet PSR J2322-2650b, which orbits a pulsar? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA’s Webb Observes Exoplanet Whose Composition Defies Explanation](https://science.nasa.gov/missions/webb/nasas-webb-observes-exoplanet-whose-composition-defies-explanation/) | - Atmosphere is dominated by helium and carbon, unlike any seen before<br>- It is the only known exoplanet with detectable molecular carbon<br>- It is the only gas-giant-like planet known orbiting a pulsar | Hard: requires synthesizing several distinctive claims from one article, not just one number. |
| ☐ | eval-004 | What evidence suggests exoplanet 29 Cygni b formed like a planet rather than a star? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA's Webb Redefines Dividing Line Between Planets, Stars](https://science.nasa.gov/missions/webb/nasas-webb-redefines-dividing-line-between-planets-stars/) | - Ground-based CHARA interferometer found the planet's orbit is aligned with its star's spin<br>- That alignment indicates formation via disk accretion, not gas fragmentation<br>- The planet is enriched in heavy elements equivalent to about 150 Earths' worth of metals | Hard: the key evidence is a specific interferometry finding, easy to miss or oversimplify. |
| ☐ | eval-005 | What is notable about the redshift Webb confirmed for galaxy MoM-z14, and how does it compare to the previous distance record holder? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA Webb Pushes Boundaries of Observable Universe Closer to Big Bang](https://science.nasa.gov/missions/webb/nasa-webb-pushes-boundaries-of-observable-universe-closer-to-big-bang/) | - MoM-z14 has a confirmed redshift of 14.44<br>- It existed roughly 280 million years after the Big Bang<br>- The previous record holder, GN-z11, existed about 400 million years after the Big Bang | Medium: combines a specific number with a comparison mentioned in the same article. |
| ☐ | eval-006 | How did NASA's Webb telescope help identify the progenitor star of supernova 2025pht? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA’s Webb Telescope Locates Former Star That Exploded as Supernova](https://science.nasa.gov/missions/webb/nasas-webb-telescope-locates-former-star-that-exploded-as-supernova/) | - Pre-existing Webb images from 2024 (MIRI and NIRCam) had already imaged the progenitor star<br>- The progenitor was an unusually red, dusty red supergiant star<br>- It was the first published detection of a supernova progenitor by Webb | Medium: tests whether retrieval finds the specific mechanism, not just that Webb was involved. |
| ☐ | eval-007 | What new JPL technology let the Perseverance rover pinpoint its own location on Mars for the first time without help from Earth, and how accurate is it? | retrieval | retrieval | [Perseverance Pinpoints Its Location at ‘Mala Mala’](https://science.nasa.gov/photojournal/perseverance-pinpoints-its-location-at-mala-mala/) | - The technology is called Mars Global Localization<br>- It determines location within about 10 inches (25 centimeters)<br>- The process takes about two minutes and reuses a processor originally built for Ingenuity communications | Medium: note that mars-global-localization-pinpoints-perseverance-s-location-cf260dea covers the identical event/facts as a near-duplicate release. |
| ☐ | eval-008 | What distance milestone did Perseverance reach in June 2026, and how does that compare to the previous record holder? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA’s HiRISE Captures Perseverance Marking a Milestone on Mars](https://science.nasa.gov/photojournal/nasas-hirise-captures-perseverance-marking-a-milestone-on-mars/) | - Perseverance reached a marathon distance of 26.2 miles (42.195 km)<br>- It took Perseverance 5 years and 4 months to reach that distance<br>- The previous record holder, Opportunity, took 11 years and 2 months to reach the same distance | Medium: requires the comparison, not just the milestone. |
| ☐ | eval-009 | What curious finding about dark rocks near Curiosity's 'land of the polygons' terrain raised the question of whether they might be meteorites? | retrieval | retrieval | [Curiosity Blog, Sols 4934-4940: In the Land of the Polygons](https://science.nasa.gov/blog/curiosity-blog-sols-4934-4940-in-the-land-of-the-polygons/) | - Dark 'float' rocks in the area were found to contain nickel<br>- Nickel is common in meteorites but less common in typical Martian rocks<br>- This raised an open question about whether the dark pebble/cobble-sized rocks are meteorites | Hard: a nuanced scientific inference buried in a mission blog post, not a headline fact. |
| ☐ | eval-010 | How deep did Curiosity drill at the 'Campo Marte' target, and how does that compare to its usual drilling depth? | retrieval | retrieval | [Curiosity Blog, Sols 4900-4907: Pasadena, We Have a Drill Sample!](https://science.nasa.gov/blog/curiosity-blog-sols-4900-4907-pasadena-we-have-a-drill-sample/) | - Curiosity drilled to a depth of about 28 mm (roughly 1.1 inches)<br>- That is shallower than its usual drilling depth of about 35 mm (1.38 inches)<br>- One sample portion went to the CheMin instrument; four more were planned for SAM | Medium: a specific numeric comparison from a mission blog. |
| ☐ | eval-011 | Why are the rocks along Jezero Crater's rim considered especially scientifically valuable? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA’s Perseverance Mars Rover Surveys ‘Crocodile Bridge’](https://science.nasa.gov/photojournal/nasas-perseverance-mars-rover-surveys-crocodile-bridge/) | - They are among the oldest rocks in the solar system<br>- They are preserved because Mars, unlike Earth, lacks tectonic plates that recycle surface material | Medium: explanatory reasoning rather than a single factoid. |
| ☐ | eval-012 | How many images make up the 3D anaglyph version of Perseverance's panorama at 'Arbot'? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA’s Perseverance Captures Panorama at ‘Arbot’](https://science.nasa.gov/photojournal/nasas-perseverance-captures-panorama-at-arbot/) | - The 3D anaglyph version (Figure B) is composed of 92 Mastcam-Z images<br>- The panorama was captured April 5, 2026 (sol 1,882) | Easy, but deliberately similar-sounding to the westernmost-selfie case (61 images) - tests retrieval precision against a near-duplicate topic. |
| ☐ | eval-013 | What was significant about CAPSTONE's role as the first U.S. commercial mission at the Moon? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA’s CAPSTONE Completes Extended Mission Testing Lunar Technologies](https://www.nasa.gov/technology/space-comms/nasas-capstone-completes-extended-mission-testing-lunar-technologies/) | - CAPSTONE was the first spacecraft to fly and characterize a near-rectilinear halo (three-body) orbit<br>- It received a 15-month mission extension to test autonomous navigation (autoNGC) and delay/disruption tolerant networking | Medium. |
| ☐ | eval-014 | About how many acres of wooded land did NASA transfer from its Goddard Space Flight Center to the Patuxent Research Refuge? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA Transfers ‘Hundred Acre Wood’ to Patuxent Research Refuge](https://www.nasa.gov/centers-and-facilities/goddard/nasa-transfers-hundred-acre-wood-to-patuxent-research-refuge/) | - About 105 acres of wooded land<br>- The land was formerly known as 'Area 400' and was used for propellant research | Easy, single number. |
| ☐ | eval-015 | How long was New Horizons' most recent hibernation period before it woke on June 23, and how far is the spacecraft from Earth? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA’s New Horizons Spacecraft Wakes from Hibernation in Good Health](https://science.nasa.gov/missions/new-horizons/nasas-new-horizons-spacecraft-wakes-from-hibernation-in-good-health/) | - The hibernation lasted 321 days, its longest yet<br>- The spacecraft is about 5.9 billion miles (9.5 billion km) from Earth<br>- Its signal took about 8 hours 52 minutes to reach Earth | Medium: two distinct numbers in the same article. |
| ☐ | eval-016 | How large was the Cottonwood fire in Utah as of July 7, 2026, and what NASA tool was used to track it? | retrieval | retrieval | [Cottonwood Fire Chars Utah](https://science.nasa.gov/earth/earth-observatory/cottonwood-fire-chars-utah/) | - The fire had burned 150 square miles (390 square kilometers)<br>- NASA's FEDS (Fire Events Data Suite) was used to track it<br>- FEDS uses VIIRS sensors aboard the Suomi NPP, NOAA-20, and NOAA-21 satellites | Medium. |
| ☐ | eval-017 | What water-level and temperature thresholds were associated with harmful algal blooms at Blue Mesa Reservoir, according to USGS research? | retrieval | retrieval | [Examining Algal Blooms in Blue Mesa](https://science.nasa.gov/earth/earth-observatory/examining-algal-blooms-in-blue-mesa/) | - Blooms were more common when water levels fell below 7,470 feet<br>- Blooms were more common when water temperatures rose above about 19.5°C (67.1°F) | Hard: precise numeric thresholds, easy to get approximately-but-not-exactly right. |
| ☐ | eval-018 | Which two companies were selected under NASA's Commercial Crew Transportation Capability (CCtCap) contract announced in September 2014? | retrieval | retrieval | [The NASA Commercial Crew Program (CCP) Mission Assurance Process](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/20160006488/downloads/20160006488.pdf) | - Boeing<br>- SpaceX | Easy, named-entity recall. |
| ☐ | eval-019 | What were the call signs of the Apollo 10 command module and lunar module, and what was distinctive about them? | retrieval | retrieval | [Mission report - Apollo 10](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/19690020110/downloads/19690020110.pdf) | - The command module was called 'Charlie Brown'<br>- The lunar module was called 'Snoopy'<br>- Both were named after Peanuts comic strip characters<br>- Snoopy descended to within 8.4 nautical miles of the lunar surface | Medium: from an older, denser mission report document. |
| ☐ | eval-020 | Who was the first American to orbit Earth, and how many orbits did he complete? | retrieval | retrieval | [This is NASA](https://ntrs.nasa.gov/api/citations/19650019449/downloads/19650019449.pdf) | - John H. Glenn Jr. was the first American to orbit Earth<br>- He completed 3 orbits<br>- This happened February 20, 1962 | Easy, general NASA history fact from a vintage booklet document. |
| ☐ | eval-021 | How much did the rock that got stuck on Curiosity's drill in 2026 weigh, and how did the team eventually free it? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA’s Curiosity Rover Frees Its Drill From a Rock](https://science.nasa.gov/photojournal/nasas-curiosity-rover-frees-its-drill-from-a-rock/)<br>[Curiosity Blog, Sols 4879-4885: Struggle at Atacama](https://science.nasa.gov/blog/curiosity-blog-sols-4879-4885-struggle-at-atacama/) | - The rock weighed roughly 28.6 pounds (13 kilograms)<br>- The rock was freed on May 1, 2026, after an earlier failed attempt on April 29<br>- It was freed through reorientation of the arm and percussion/vibration of the drill | Multi-doc: the photojournal release gives the weight, the mission blog gives the freeing process/timeline. |
| ☐ | eval-022 | How do Webb's atmospheric findings compare between TRAPPIST-1 d and TRAPPIST-1 e? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA Webb Looks at Earth-Sized, Habitable-Zone Exoplanet TRAPPIST-1 e](https://science.nasa.gov/missions/webb/nasa-webb-looks-at-earth-sized-habitable-zone-exoplanet-trappist-1-e/)<br>[Webb Narrows Atmospheric Possibilities for Earth-sized Exoplanet TRAPPIST-1 d](https://science.nasa.gov/missions/webb/webb-narrows-atmospheric-possibilities-for-earth-sized-exoplanet-trappist-1-d/) | - For TRAPPIST-1 d, Webb ruled out water, methane, and CO2, ruling out an Earth-like atmosphere<br>- For TRAPPIST-1 e, researchers believe any primary hydrogen-helium atmosphere was stripped by stellar radiation, and a secondary atmosphere is still uncertain<br>- Neither planet has a confirmed atmosphere yet | Multi-doc: requires combining two companion Webb studies of adjacent TRAPPIST-1 planets. |
| ☐ | eval-023 | What was distinctive about the December 10, 2025 Perseverance rover drive along the Jezero Crater rim, and how was it visualized? | retrieval | retrieval | [Video: Perseverance Rover’s View of Crater Rim Drive](https://science.nasa.gov/photojournal/video-perseverance-rovers-view-of-crater-rim-drive/)<br>[Visualizing Perseverance’s AI-Planned Drive on Mars](https://science.nasa.gov/photojournal/visualizing-perseverances-ai-planned-drive-on-mars/) | - The drive covered 807 feet (246 meters) along the crater rim on sol 1,709<br>- Generative AI provided the route planning for this drive<br>- One reconstruction used Navcam image pairs in a 3D environment; another used the 'Caspian' visualization tool | Multi-doc: two releases describe the same drive from different visualization angles. |
| ☐ | eval-024 | What evidence has Webb found for supermassive black holes forming very early in the universe, across different 'little red dot' galaxies? | retrieval | retrieval | [NASA’s Webb Reveals Black Hole That Formed Before Its Galaxy](https://science.nasa.gov/missions/webb/nasas-webb-reveals-black-hole-that-formed-before-its-galaxy/)<br>[NASA Webb Finds Strongest Evidence Yet for ‘Black Hole Stars’](https://science.nasa.gov/missions/webb/nasa-webb-finds-strongest-evidence-yet-for-black-hole-stars/) | - QSO1 existed just 700 million years after the Big Bang, with a black hole mass of about 50 million solar masses measured via Keplerian gas motion<br>- GLIMPSE-17775 existed about 1.8 billion years after the Big Bang; a deep 30-hour spectrum revealed an 'iron forest' supporting the 'black hole star' model<br>- Both are examples of gravitationally lensed 'little red dot' galaxies | Multi-doc, hard: requires synthesizing two separate black-hole discovery articles. |
| ☐ | eval-025 | Where is the ISS right now? | tools | tools | — | - Reports a current latitude and longitude for the ISS | Expects the iss_now tool. |
| ☐ | eval-026 | What is today's astronomy picture of the day? | tools | tools | — | - Reports a title, image/video, and explanation for a specific date's APOD | Expects the apod tool (no date given, defaults to today). |
| ☐ | eval-027 | Show me the most recent photos from the Curiosity rover. | tools | tools | — | - Reports one or more Mars rover photos with camera name and Earth date, attributed to Curiosity | Expects the mars_rover_photos tool with no date args (latest photos). |
| ☐ | eval-028 | Show me a JWST image of the Carina Nebula. | tools | tools | — | - Reports one or more JWST-related images/media with a title and description mentioning the Carina Nebula | Expects the jwst_images tool, not search_documents (imagery lookup, not a science question). |
| ☐ | eval-029 | What's the current latitude and longitude of the International Space Station? | tools | tools | — | - Reports a current latitude and longitude for the ISS | Rephrased version of eval-025 to check routing consistency across wording; expects iss_now. |
| ☐ | eval-030 | Get me NASA's astronomy picture of the day for July 1, 2026. | tools | tools | — | - Reports the APOD specifically for 2026-07-01, including title and explanation | Expects the apod tool with an explicit date argument. |
| ☐ | eval-031 | Show me Perseverance rover photos from Mars. | tools | tools | — | - Reports one or more Mars rover photos attributed to Perseverance | Expects the mars_rover_photos tool. |
| ☐ | eval-032 | Find me JWST pictures related to exoplanets. | tools | tools | — | - Reports one or more JWST-related images/media whose titles or descriptions relate to exoplanets | Expects the jwst_images tool, not search_documents. |
| ☐ | eval-033 | Show me a JWST image of a supernova, and explain what Webb discovered about the star that exploded as supernova 2025pht. | both | both | [NASA’s Webb Telescope Locates Former Star That Exploded as Supernova](https://science.nasa.gov/missions/webb/nasas-webb-telescope-locates-former-star-that-exploded-as-supernova/) | - Reports a JWST-related image/media result for a supernova<br>- Explains that Webb's pre-existing 2024 images identified the progenitor as an unusually red, dusty red supergiant star<br>- Notes this was the first published Webb detection of a supernova progenitor | Both: jwst_images for the picture, search_documents for the science explanation. |
| ☐ | eval-034 | Show me the most recent Perseverance rover photos, and tell me about the technology that helps Perseverance figure out its own location on Mars. | both | both | [Perseverance Pinpoints Its Location at ‘Mala Mala’](https://science.nasa.gov/photojournal/perseverance-pinpoints-its-location-at-mala-mala/) | - Reports one or more recent Perseverance photos<br>- Explains Mars Global Localization: determines location to within about 10 inches (25 cm) in about two minutes | Both: mars_rover_photos for the imagery, search_documents for the technology background. |
| ☐ | eval-035 | What is today's astronomy picture of the day, and has Webb studied any exoplanets with extreme temperature swings like HD 80606 b? | both | both | [NASA's Webb Catches Exoplanet Getting Roasted](https://science.nasa.gov/missions/webb/nasas-webb-catches-exoplanet-getting-roasted/) | - Reports today's APOD (title, explanation)<br>- Explains that Webb observed HD 80606 b's temperature spike by about 1,100°F near periastron | Both: apod for the picture of the day, search_documents for the HD 80606 b background. |
| ☐ | eval-036 | Where is the ISS right now, and what has NASA said about future commercial space stations replacing it? | both | both | [NASA Seeks Industry Input on Second Phase of Commercial Space Stations](https://www.nasa.gov/humans-in-space/commercial-space/nasa-seeks-industry-input-on-second-phase-of-commercial-space-stations/) | - Reports a current latitude and longitude for the ISS<br>- Mentions NASA's draft RFP for the next phase of its commercial space stations strategy, transitioning activities from the ISS | Both: iss_now for position, search_documents for the commercial-stations transition context. |
